comments.author: niole
comments.body: This statement makes it so that if someone disables the service, but it happens to still be running, you won't be able to stop the search event stream. THis could mean that this service would get stuck in a running state, when it should be stopped

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: that's not possible, it can only be disabled by restarting the pod, which means it won't be running anymore.

pr_title: [DOM-54217] move develop scheduled / periodic tasks into nucleus-develop deployment

comments.author: niole
comments.body: It sounds like this issue should be solved through a mongo db migration that removes the quartz job. I don't think it's necessary to write backwards compatible logic in scala for this

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: this has been examined by me back when i did similar logic and again by me and martin with involvement from distributions for this PR. a simple mongo migration wont work, this logic is necessary.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: for some reason I thought that the quartz job metadata was kept in mongo. just for my own understanding: is it not kept in mongo or is it something else about the job which keeps a mongo migration from resolving this?

comments.author: ddl-mhito
comments.body:
From Ryan when we had this discussion earlier: 

> - we upgrade and the migrations run.
> - so, now the job record is removed from the mongo quartz job collection.
> however, after that but before nucleus pod upgrade happens, the nucleus-dispatcher restarts (for whatever reason), still running its pre-upgrade code.
> - well, that pre upgrade code automatically registers the job on app start if it is not found in the quartz mongo collection.
> - now the nucleus pod image upgrade actually happens. but since we removed the quartz job code instead of telling the quartz job to unregister if it exists, now the job lives in the quartz mongo collection forever, being triggered normally, unless there is manual intervention.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: "well, that pre upgrade code automatically registers the job on app start if it is not found in the quartz mongo collection.", should this code be removed?
